channel.title: Best Practices on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Best Practices on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Transfer Learning (12): Industrial Applications and Best Practices
item.description: A three-person team at a fintech startup shipped a fraud-detection model in two weeks that outperformed the previous system built by 12 engineers over 6 months. The secret? They fine-tuned a pretrained transformer on 5,000 labeled transactions instead of architecting a rule-based ensemble from scratch. The model caught 23% more fraud in the first month while cutting false positives in half. When their VP of Engineering asked why the old team took so long, the answer was simple: they didn’t have transfer learning.

item.title: Docker and Containers (7): Security — Running Containers Without Giving Away the Keys
item.description:
Docker’s default configuration prioritizes convenience over security. Out of the box, containers run as root, have access to a broad set of Linux capabilities, and can write to their entire filesystem. This is fine for development — dangerous for production. A container escape vulnerability combined with a root-privileged container means an attacker owns the host. Let’s fix that.

The Threat Model

Before locking things down, understand what you’re defending against:
